title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: If @ = -, $ = ÷, % = +, # = ×, then 18 % 6 @ 4 # 12 $ 3 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: 18 % 6 @ 4 # 12 $ 3 = ? Given Symbol @ $ % # New Sign - ÷ + × Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ New equation: 18 + 6 - 4 × 12 ÷ 3 = ?​​ 18 + 6 - 4 × 4 = ? 18 + 6 - 16 = ? 24 - 16 = ? ? = 8 Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: If @ = +, $ = ÷, % = ×, then find the value of: 12 % 2 $ 3 @ 4?
questions.explanation: Given: 12 % 2 $ 3 @ 4? Given Symbol @ $ % New Sign + ÷ × Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[],{}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\ \hline \text{Addition} & + \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ New equation: ​12 × 2 ÷ 3 + 4 = ? 24 ÷ 3 + 4 = ? 8 + 4 = ? ? = 12 Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: If each letter is coded as its position in reverse (Z = 1, Y = 2, …, A = 26), what is COLD?
questions.explanation: Given: If each letter is coded as its position in reverse (Z = 1, Y = 2, …, A = 26). For, COLD - ? C = X = 24 O = L = 12 L = O = 15 D = W = 23 So, COLD is coded as 24 12 15 23. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Which of the following letter-clusters should replace # and % so that the pattern and relationship followed between the letter-cluster pair on the left side of :: is the same as that on the right side of :: ? # : OWF :: VDM : %
questions.explanation: Given: ​# : OWF :: VDM : % Logic: Each letter in the first cluster is shifted 5 positions backward to get the second cluster​ Step-by-step: From option C: = TBK T → O (−5) B → W (−5, cyclic) K → F (−5) So TBK : OWF Apply same to VDM: V → Q (−5) D → Y (−5, cyclic) M → H (−5) => % = QYH Final Answer: = TBK, % = QYH Final Correct Option: (C)

questions.question: In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Sandstone : Rawasthan :: Limestone : ?
questions.explanation: Given: Sandstone : Rawasthan :: Limestone : ? Sandstone is abundantly found in Rawasthan. Similarly, Limestone : ? Limestone is abundantly found in Meghalaya.​ Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Select the correct letter-cluster to replace (?). AEEI, BFJN, CGOS, DHTX, ?
questions.explanation: Given: AEEI, BFJN, CGOS, DHTX, ? Logic: 1st letter and 2nd letter + 1, 3rd letter + 5 and 4th letter + 5 place. For, 1st and 2nd letter A + 1 = B, B + 1 = C, C + 1 = D, D + 1 = E E + 1 = F, F + 1 = G, G + 1 = H, H + 1 = I For, 3rd and 4th letter E + 5 = J, J + 5 = O, O + 5 = T, T + 5 = Y I + 5 = N, N + 5 = S, S + 5 = X, X + 5 = C So, the missing term is EIYC. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Statements: 1. All cats are animals. 2. Some animals are wild. Conclusions: I. Some cats are wild. II. Some animals are cats.
questions.explanation: Statements: 1. All cats are animals. 2. Some animals are wild. From the given statements possible Venn diagram will be. Conclusions: I. Some cats are wild. (False, there is no relation between cats and wild). II. Some animals are cats. (True, all cats are animals, so that means some animals are cats). So, ​Only II follows. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which word cannot be formed using the letters of CONVERSATION?
questions.explanation: Given: CONVERSATION Letters in CONVERSATION: C, O, N, V, E, R, S, A, T, I, O, N (Note: only one “O” and one “N” each appear twice; no extra letters) Now, we check each options. Option (a): Conservation (Formed) Letters needed: C O N S E R V A T I O N All letters are present ​Option (b): Reaction (Formed)​ Letters needed: R E A C T I O N All letters available ​Option (c): Overtone (Not Formed)​ Letters needed: O V E R T O N E Requires E = 2 times In CONVERSATION, E appears only once ​Option (d): Orient (Formed)​ Letters needed: O R I E N T A T I O N All letters available So, Overtone cannot be formed. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: From the word “BRIGHT”, how many meaningful English words can be formed using all the letters exactly once?
questions.explanation: Given: BRIGHT Letters: B, R, I, G, H, T We must form meaningful English words using all six letters exactly once. Check possible words: BRIGHT Uses B R I G H T → all letters used once So, one meaningful English words can be formed using all the letters exactly once. Thus, correcet option is (a).

questions.question: R is to V as K is to:
questions.explanation: Given: R is to V as K is to: Logic: Letters are increasing + 4 place. For, R - V R + 4 = V Similarly, K - ? K + 4 = O So, K is to O. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: If ‘A’ stands for ‘÷’, ‘B’ stands for ‘×’, ‘C’ stands for ‘+’ and ‘D’ stands for ‘−’, then what will come in place of question mark (?) in the following equation? 147 A 7 C 104 D 17 B 6 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: ​147 A 7 C 104 D 17 B 6 = ? Letter A B C D Sign ÷ × + - ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction−\begin{array}{|c|c|} \hline\textbf{Operation preference wise} & \textbf{Symbol} \\\hline\text{Brackets} &[], {}, () \\ \hline \text{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \\ \hline \text{Division}& ÷ \\ \hline \text{Multiplication} & × \\\hline \text{Addition} &+ \\ \hline \text{Subtraction} & - \\\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ After using the sign equation will be:​ 147 ÷ 7 + 104 − 17 × 6 = ? ? = 21 + 104 − 17 × 6 ? = 21 + 104 − 102 ? = 125 − 102 ? = 23 Thus, the correct option is: (a)​​

questions.question: Identify the assumption: “The company extended store hours to improve customer satisfaction.”
options: Longer hours will attract more customers
Employees agreed to work extra hours
questions.explanation: Assumption: “The company extended store hours to improve customer satisfaction.” Explanation: The statement assumes that extending store hours will lead to improved customer satisfaction, which is only possible if customers are willing to shop during the extended hours and that this will benefit them.​ So, Longer hours will attract more customers. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Find the odd one out.
questions.explanation: Logic: 1st number × 2nd number = 3rd number Now, we check each options. ​Option (a): ​8 @ 2 = 16 8 × 2 = 16 ​Option (b): ​6 @ 3 = 18 6 × 3 = 18 ​Option (c): ​5 @ 4 = 20 5 × 4 = 20 ​Option (d): ​7 @ 2 = 21 7 × 2 ≠\ne= 21​ Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: A family consists of a grandmother, her husband, their three sons, each son’s wife, and one son per son. Find total male and female members.
questions.explanation: Given: A family consists of a grandmother, her husband, their three sons, each son’s wife, and one son per son. From the given information blood relation diagram will be. So, total male and female members are 7 males, 4 females. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: From the letters of the word "PRODUCT", how many distinct 4-letter words can be formed if the word must start and end with a vowel?
questions.explanation: Given: Word: PRODUCT (all letters distinct) Vowels: O, U Condition: 4-letter word must start and end with a vowel, no repetition Concept Used: Permutation without repetition Start and end positions: O _ _ U U _ _ O Formula Used: ​nPr=n!(n−r)!^nP_r = \frac{n!}{(n−r)!}nPr​=(n−r)!n!​​​ Solution: There are 2 vowels O and U. They must occupy the first and last positions without repetition. Remaining letters are 5 consonants: P, R, D, C, T. Fill the middle two positions using these consonants: Number of ways = 5 × 4 = 20 Total number of words = 2 × 20 = 40

questions.question: If NOTE is written as PQVG, how is BOOK written?
questions.explanation: Given: If NOTE is written as PQVG. Logic: Letters are increasing + 2 place. For, NOTE - PQVG N + 2 = P, O + 2 = Q, T + 2 = V, E + 2 = G Similarly, BOOK - ? B + 2 = D, O + 2 = Q, O + 2 = Q, K + 2 = M So, BOOK is written as DQQM. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Find the missing term: 5, 18, 77, ___, 2361
questions.explanation: Given: 5, 18, 77, ___, 2361 Logic: Numbers are multiply by natural number from 3 and add odd number from 3. 5 × 3 + 3 = 18 18 × 4 + 5 = 77 77 × 5 + 7 = 392 392 × 6 + 9 = 2361 So, the missing term is 392. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In a 48 L mixture, the ratio of milk to water is 3 : 5. How much water must be removed to make the ratio 1 : 1?
questions.explanation: Given: In a 48 L mixture, the ratio of milk to water is 3 : 5. Solution: Total parts = 3 + 5 = 8 Milk = (3/8) × 48 = 18 L Water = (5/8) × 48 = 30 L For ratio 1 : 1 → Water must become 18 L Required water to remove excess = 30 − 18 = 12 L So, 12 L water must be added to make the ratio 1 : 1. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: ​Adhaar and Pan are sisters who have Parliament as their mother. Money has two brothers, Coin and Notes. Parliament's husband News is also the father of Money. How is Pan related to Money?​
questions.explanation: Given: Adhaar and Pan are sisters who have Parliament as their mother. Money has two brothers, Coin and Notes. Parliament's husband News is also the father of Money. Symbol Meaning: ​Symbol in DiagramMeaning− /◯Female + / □Male = Married Couple _ Siblings| Difference of Generation\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline\textbf{Symbol in Diagram} & \textbf{Meaning} \\ \hline \bf - \ / \bf\bigcirc & \text{Female} \\ \hline\text{ \bf+ \ / \ }\bf\square & \text{Male} \\ \hline\text{ \bf= } & \text{Married Couple} \\ \hline\text{ \bf\_ } & \text{Siblings} \\ \hline\text{\bf | } & \text{Difference of Generation} \\ \hline\end{array}Symbol in Diagram− /◯ + / □ = _ | ​MeaningFemaleMaleMarried CoupleSiblingsDifference of Generation​​​​ From the given information family tree will be given as: From the given figure we can say that Pan is the sister of Money. Hence option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Pointing to a woman in a party, Suman said, “Her father-in-law’s only son is my only brother’s father”. How is the woman related to Suman?
questions.explanation: Given - Pointing to a woman in a party, Suman said, “Her father-in-law’s only son is my only brother’s father”. the woman (Lady) is suman's mother.

questions.question: Six students are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Kapil is sitting second to the left of Tia. Bhanu is sitting third to the right of Piya. Ritu is an immediate neighbour of both Tia and Bhanu. Kapil is an immediate neighbour of Arwun and Piya. Who is sitting between Bhanu and Kapil?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Six students are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Kapil is sitting second to the left of Tia. Bhanu is sitting third to the right of Piya. Ritu is an immediate neighbour of both Tia and Bhanu. Kapil is an immediate neighbour of Arwun and Piya. From the given information seating arrangement will be: So, Arwun is sitting between Bhanu and Kapil. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: Statement: “The bridge was closed due to structural damage.” Conclusions: I. The bridge was unsafe. II. All bridges are unsafe.​​
questions.explanation: Statement: “The bridge was closed due to structural damage.” Conclusions: I. The bridge was unsafe. If a bridge has structural damage and is closed, it clearly means it was unsafe to use. II. All bridges are unsafe. This is a general statement about all bridges, which is not supported. So, Only I follows. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: If TIGER is coded as SHFDQ, how is LION written?
questions.explanation: Given: If TIGER is coded as SHFDQ. Logic: Letters are decreasing - 1 place. For, TIGER - SHFDQ T - 1 = S, I - 1 = H, G - 1 = F, E - 1 = D, R - 1 = Q Similarly, LION - ? L - 1 = K, I - 1 = H, O - 1 = N, N - 1 = M So, LION is coded as KHNM. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: The number of subscribers increases by 20% yearly: 2000, 2400, 2880, 3456. What is the 5th year?
questions.explanation: Given: The number of subscribers increases by 20% yearly: 2000, 2400, 2880, 3456. The number increases by 20% every year, i.e. multiply by 1.2 each time. 2000 → 2400 → 2880 → 3456 5th year: 3456 × 1.2 = 4147.2 So, 4147 is the 5th year. Thus, correctoption is (b).

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? XQS, VOQ, TMO, RKM, ?
questions.explanation: Given: XQS, VOQ, TMO, RKM, ? Logic: 1st, 2nd, 3rd letters are decreasing by 2 each time. 1st letters: X −2 = V, V −2 = T, T −2 = R, R −2 = P 2nd letters: Q −2 = O, O −2 = M, M −2 = K, K −2 = I 3rd letters: S −2 = Q, Q −2 = O, O −2 = M, M −2 = K So, the next term is PIK. Thus, the correct option is (c).
